content.content:
1	#!/bin/bash
2	#
3	# Copyright 2025 Scale Invariant, Inc.
4	#
5	# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
6	# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
7	# You may obtain a copy of the License at
8	#
9	#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
10	#
11	# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
12	# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
13	# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
14	# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
15	# limitations under the License.
16	#
17	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
18	#
19	# RBOB - Recipe Bottle Orchestration Bottle
20	# Container lifecycle management via Docker Compose
21	#
22	# Requires: buc_command.sh sourced
23	# Requires: rbrn_regime.sh sourced
24	# Requires: rbrr_regime.sh sourced
25	
26	set -euo pipefail
27	
28	# Multiple inclusion detection
29	test -z "${ZRBOB_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbob multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
30	ZRBOB_SOURCED=1
31	
32	
33	######################################################################
34	# Compose-env Validation
35	
36	# Validate that compose-consumed RBRN fields contain no quotes or ${VAR} references.
37	# Compose --env-file does not strip quotes (they become part of the value) and
38	# interpolates ${VAR} references (causing unintended expansion). Fields not consumed
39	# by compose (e.g., RBRN_DESCRIPTION) are exempt.
40	zrbob_validate_compose_env() {
41	  # Explicit list of compose-consumed field names (the nameplate↔compose contract)
42	  local z_compose_fields="
43	    RBRN_MONIKER
44	    RBRN_RUNTIME
45	    RBRN_SENTRY_VESSEL
46	    RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL
47	    RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK
48	    RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK
49	    RBRN_ENTRY_MODE
50	    RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION
51	    RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE
52	    RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP
53	    RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK
54	    RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP
55	    RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP
56	    RBRN_UPLINK_PORT_MIN
57	    RBRN_UPLINK_DNS_MODE
58	    RBRN_UPLINK_ACCESS_MODE
59	    RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_CIDRS
60	    RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_DOMAINS
61	  "
62	
63	  local z_field=""
64	  for z_field in ${z_compose_fields}; do
65	    local z_value="${!z_field:-}"
66	    # Check for quotes (compose does not strip them)
67	    case "${z_value}" in
68	      *\"*|*\'*)
69	        buc_die "Compose compatibility: ${z_field} contains quotes — compose --env-file does not strip quotes, they become part of the value"
70	        ;;
71	    esac
72	    # Check for ${VAR} references (compose interpolates them)
73	    case "${z_value}" in
74	      *'${'*)
75	        buc_die "Compose compatibility: ${z_field} contains \${VAR} reference — compose --env-file interpolates these, causing unintended expansion"
76	        ;;
77	    esac
78	  done
79	}
80	
81	######################################################################
82	# Kindle and Sentinel
83	
84	zrbob_kindle() {
85	  test -z "${ZRBOB_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbob already kindled"
86	
87	  # Verify RBRN regime is kindled (provides nameplate config)
88	  zrbrn_sentinel
89	
90	  # Validate compose-consumed fields before proceeding
91	  zrbob_validate_compose_env
92	
93	  # Verify RBRR regime is kindled (provides repo config like DNS_SERVER)
94	  zrbrr_sentinel
95	
96	  # Runtime command (docker or podman)
97	  case "${RBRN_RUNTIME}" in
98	    docker) readonly ZRBOB_RUNTIME="docker" ;;
99	    podman) readonly ZRBOB_RUNTIME="podman" ;;
100	    *) buc_die "Unknown RBRN_RUNTIME: ${RBRN_RUNTIME}" ;;
101	  esac
102	
103	  # Compose project identity for this nameplate. The runtime-prefixed moniker
104	  # is the authoritative project name compose labels containers and networks
105	  # under; every -p invocation and every container/network name derives from it.
106	  readonly ZRBOB_PROJECT="${RBRR_RUNTIME_PREFIX}${RBRN_MONIKER}"
107	
108	  # Container names (for connect and info commands)
109	  readonly ZRBOB_SENTRY="${ZRBOB_PROJECT}-sentry"
110	  readonly ZRBOB_PENTACLE="${ZRBOB_PROJECT}-pentacle"
111	  readonly ZRBOB_BOTTLE="${ZRBOB_PROJECT}-bottle"
112	
113	  # Network name (compose names as {project}_{network}; used by observe and info)
114	  readonly ZRBOB_NETWORK="${ZRBOB_PROJECT}_enclave"
115	
116	  # Compose file paths (relative to project root, where compose runs)
117	  readonly ZRBOB_COMPOSE_BASE="${RBBC_dot_dir}/rbob_compose.yml"
118	  test -f "${ZRBOB_COMPOSE_BASE}" || buc_die "Base compose file not found: ${ZRBOB_COMPOSE_BASE}"
119	
120	  readonly ZRBOB_COMPOSE_FRAGMENT="${RBBC_dot_dir}/${RBRN_MONIKER}/compose.yml"
121	  # Fragment is optional — existence checked at compose invocation time
122	
123	  # Env file paths (for compose --env-file: YAML interpolation + container env forwarding)
124	  readonly ZRBOB_ENV_RBRR="${RBBC_dot_dir}/rbrr.env"
125	  readonly ZRBOB_ENV_RBJE="${RBBC_dot_dir}/rbje_compose_probe.env"
126	  readonly ZRBOB_ENV_RBRN="${RBBC_dot_dir}/${RBRN_MONIKER}/${RBCC_rbrn_file}"
127	
128	  # RBDC env file — RBDC_* are bash-kindle constants invisible to compose
129	  # without an env-file bridge. Write the subset compose interpolates into
130	  # a temp file and feed it via --env-file.
131	  local z_rbdc_env="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbob_rbdc_compose.env"
132	  printf 'RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID=%s\nRBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY=%s\n' \
133	    "${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}" "${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY}" > "${z_rbdc_env}" \
134	    || buc_die "Failed to write RBDC compose env file: ${z_rbdc_env}"
135	  readonly ZRBOB_ENV_RBDC="${z_rbdc_env}"
136	
137	
138	  # GAR image references (computed once, used by preflight and auto-summon)
139	  local z_gar_base="${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}/${RBGD_GAR_PROJECT_ID}/${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY}"
140	  readonly ZRBOB_SENTRY_IMAGE="${z_gar_base}/${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}/${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_IMAGE}:${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}"
141	  readonly ZRBOB_BOTTLE_IMAGE="${z_gar_base}/${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/${RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK}/${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_IMAGE}:${RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK}"
142	
143	  # GAR vouch references (local presence verified on every start)
144	  readonly ZRBOB_SENTRY_VOUCH="${z_gar_base}/${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}/${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_VOUCH}:${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}"
145	  readonly ZRBOB_BOTTLE_VOUCH="${z_gar_base}/${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/${RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK}/${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_VOUCH}:${RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK}"
146	
147	  # Export RBRN/RBRR vars for compose environment: bare name forwarding.
148	  # Compose --env-file populates the compose environment for both YAML interpolation
149	  # and bare name forwarding; explicit exports provide defense-in-depth.
150	  export RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP
151	  export RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK
152	  export RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP
153	  export RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP
154	  export RBRN_ENTRY_MODE
155	  export RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION
156	  export RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE
157	  export RBRN_UPLINK_DNS_MODE
158	  export RBRN_UPLINK_PORT_MIN
159	  export RBRN_UPLINK_ACCESS_MODE
160	  export RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_CIDRS
161	  export RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_DOMAINS
162	  export RBRR_DNS_SERVER
163	  export RBRR_BOTTLE_WORKSPACE
164	
165	  # Host UID/GID for bind-mount ownership alignment.
166	  # The person running charge IS the host identity — no configuration needed.
167	  local z_uid_file="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbob_host_uid"
168	  local z_gid_file="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbob_host_gid"
169	  id -u > "${z_uid_file}" || buc_die "Failed to determine host UID"
170	  id -g > "${z_gid_file}" || buc_die "Failed to determine host GID"
171	  local z_host_uid=""
172	  z_host_uid=$(<"${z_uid_file}")
173	  local z_host_gid=""
174	  z_host_gid=$(<"${z_gid_file}")
175	  export RBOB_HOST_UID="${z_host_uid}"
176	  export RBOB_HOST_GID="${z_host_gid}"
177	
178	  # Load bottle vessel user for compose and SSH (optional — empty means image default)
179	  local z_bottle_rbrv="${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}/${RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL}/rbrv.env"
180	  local z_bottle_user=""
181	  if test -f "${z_bottle_rbrv}"; then
182	    z_bottle_user=$(grep '^RBRV_USER=' "${z_bottle_rbrv}" | head -1 | cut -d= -f2) || true
183	  fi
184	  readonly ZRBOB_BOTTLE_USER="${z_bottle_user}"
185	  export RBRV_USER="${z_bottle_user}"
186	
187	  readonly ZRBOB_DRIVE_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbob_drive_"
188	
189	  readonly ZRBOB_KINDLED=1
190	}
191	
192	zrbob_sentinel() {
193	  test "${ZRBOB_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbob not kindled - call zrbob_kindle first"
194	}
195	
196	######################################################################
197	# Compose Command Helper
198	
199	# Build and execute a compose command with standard args
200	# Usage: zrbob_compose <compose_subcommand> [args...]
201	zrbob_compose() {
202	  zrbob_sentinel
203	
204	  local z_args=()
205	  z_args+=("compose")
206	  z_args+=("--env-file" "${ZRBOB_ENV_RBRR}")
207	  z_args+=("--env-file" "${ZRBOB_ENV_RBDC}")
208	  z_args+=("--env-file" "${ZRBOB_ENV_RBJE}")
209	  z_args+=("--env-file" "${ZRBOB_ENV_RBRN}")
210	  z_args+=("-f" "${ZRBOB_COMPOSE_BASE}")
211	
212	  # Include nameplate fragment if it exists
213	  if test -f "${ZRBOB_COMPOSE_FRAGMENT}"; then
214	    z_args+=("-f" "${ZRBOB_COMPOSE_FRAGMENT}")
215	  fi
216	
217	  z_args+=("-p" "${ZRBOB_PROJECT}")
218	  z_args+=("$@")
219	
220	  buc_log_args "${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} ${z_args[*]}"
221	  "${ZRBOB_RUNTIME}" "${z_args[@]}"
222	}
223	
224	######################################################################
225	# Auto-Summon Helpers
226	
227	# Discriminate kludge hallmarks (local-only, no GAR presence) from conjure /
228	# bind / graft hallmarks. Used by charge preflight to redirect the diagnostic
229	# away from the summon path, which is guaranteed to fail for kludge stamps.
230	zrbob_hallmark_is_kludge() {
231	  case "${1}" in
232	    "${RBGC_HALLMARK_PREFIX_KLUDGE}"*) return 0 ;;
233	    *) return 1 ;;
234	  esac
235	}
236	
237	# Pull all three arks (image, about, vouch) for a hallmark to local runtime.
238	# Used when charge detects a missing local artifact and needs to bootstrap.
239	# Mirrors rbfr_summon's all-three-arks semantics; called inline rather than
240	# as a tabtarget invocation because rbob is the sole caller and avoids the
241	# launcher-dispatch round-trip.
242	zrbob_summon_full_hallmark() {
243	  local z_hallmark="${1:-}"
244	
245	  test -n "${z_hallmark}" || buc_die "zrbob_summon_full_hallmark: hallmark required"
246	
247	  local z_gar_base="${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}/${RBGD_GAR_PROJECT_ID}/${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY}"
248	  local z_image_ref="${z_gar_base}/${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/${z_hallmark}/${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_IMAGE}:${z_hallmark}"
249	  local z_about_ref="${z_gar_base}/${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/${z_hallmark}/${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_ABOUT}:${z_hallmark}"
250	  local z_vouch_ref="${z_gar_base}/${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/${z_hallmark}/${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_VOUCH}:${z_hallmark}"
251	
252	  local z_registry_host="${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}"
253	  test -f "${RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE}" || buc_die "Retriever credential not found: ${RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE}"
254	
255	  local z_token
256	  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE}") || buc_die "Failed to get OAuth token for auto-summon"
257	
258	  buc_step "Auto-summoning hallmark ${z_hallmark} (image + about + vouch)"
259	
260	  echo "${z_token}" | docker login -u oauth2accesstoken --password-stdin "https://${z_registry_host}" \
261	    || buc_die "Container registry authentication failed during auto-summon"
262	
263	  docker pull "${z_image_ref}" || buc_die "Failed to pull image ark: ${z_image_ref}"
264	  docker pull "${z_about_ref}" || buc_die "Failed to pull about ark: ${z_about_ref}"
265	  docker pull "${z_vouch_ref}" || buc_die "Failed to pull vouch ark: ${z_vouch_ref}"
266	
267	  buc_info "Auto-summoned: ${z_hallmark}"
268	}
269	
270	# Pull a single image ref to local runtime — used by the image-only auto-summon
271	# branch in rbob_charge as defense-in-depth for partial-state cases (vouch
272	# present but image missing). The full-hallmark helper above covers the common
273	# path where all arks are missing together.
274	# Usage: zrbob_vouch_gate_and_summon <vessel> <hallmark> <image_ref>
275	zrbob_vouch_gate_and_summon() {
276	  local z_vessel="${1:-}"
277	  local z_hallmark="${2:-}"
278	  local z_image_ref="${3:-}"
279	
280	  test -n "${z_vessel}"       || buc_die "zrbob_vouch_gate_and_summon: vessel required"
281	  test -n "${z_hallmark}" || buc_die "zrbob_vouch_gate_and_summon: hallmark required"
282	  test -n "${z_image_ref}"    || buc_die "zrbob_vouch_gate_and_summon: image_ref required"
283	
284	  local z_registry_host="${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}"
285	  test -f "${RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE}" || buc_die "Retriever credential not found: ${RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE}"
286	
287	  local z_token
288	  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE}") || buc_die "Failed to get OAuth token for auto-summon"
289	
290	  # Pull the image
291	  buc_step "Auto-summoning ${z_image_ref}"
292	
293	  echo "${z_token}" | docker login -u oauth2accesstoken --password-stdin "https://${z_registry_host}" \
294	    || buc_die "Container registry authentication failed during auto-summon"
295	
296	  docker pull "${z_image_ref}" || buc_die "Failed to pull image: ${z_image_ref}"
297	
298	  buc_info "Auto-summoned: ${z_image_ref}"
299	}
300	
301	######################################################################
302	# Charge Invariant — Network Collision Detection
303	
304	# Die if any compose-managed network with our nameplate exists from a foreign
305	# project. Catches pre-prefix-era residue (project=tadmor without RBRR
306	# prefix) and any other compose project whose name is the bare moniker or
307	# ends with "-{moniker}". Match is by labels — independent of how compose
308	# joined project + network into the network name.
309	zrbob_detect_nameplate_collision() {
310	  zrbob_sentinel
311	
312	  local -r z_networks_file="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/zrbob_collision_networks.txt"
313	  local -r z_networks_stderr="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/zrbob_collision_networks_stderr.txt"
314	  local -r z_inspect_prefix="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/zrbob_collision_inspect_"
315	
316	  ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} network ls \
317	    --filter 'label=com.docker.compose.project' \
318	    --filter 'label=com.docker.compose.network' \
319	    --format '{{.Name}}' \
320	    > "${z_networks_file}" 2>"${z_networks_stderr}" \
321	    || buc_die "Failed to list Docker networks for collision detection — see ${z_networks_stderr}"
322	
323	  local z_names=()
324	  local z_line=""
325	  while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
326	    test -n "${z_line}" || continue
327	    z_names+=("${z_line}")
328	  done < "${z_networks_file}"
329	
330	  local z_offenders=()
331	  local z_index=0
332	  local z_name=""
333	  local z_inspect_file=""
334	  local z_inspect_stderr=""
335	  local z_labels=""
336	  local z_net_label=""
337	  local z_proj_label=""
338	
339	  if (( ${#z_names[@]} )); then
340	    for z_name in "${z_names[@]}"; do
341	      z_inspect_file="${z_inspect_prefix}${z_index}.txt"
342	      z_inspect_stderr="${z_inspect_prefix}${z_index}_stderr.txt"
343	      z_index=$((z_index + 1))
344	
345	      ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} network inspect "${z_name}" \
346	        --format '{{index .Labels "com.docker.compose.network"}}|{{index .Labels "com.docker.compose.project"}}' \
347	        > "${z_inspect_file}" 2>"${z_inspect_stderr}" \
348	        || continue
349	
350	      z_labels=$(<"${z_inspect_file}")
351	      z_net_label="${z_labels%%|*}"
352	      z_proj_label="${z_labels#*|}"
353	
354	      # Network must be enclave or transit
355	      test "${z_net_label}" = "enclave" || test "${z_net_label}" = "transit" \
356	        || continue
357	
358	      # Skip our own current project; compose-down already removed it (defensive)
359	      test "${z_proj_label}" != "${ZRBOB_PROJECT}" || continue
360	
361	      # Match: bare moniker, or any prefix ending in "-{moniker}"
362	      if test "${z_proj_label}" = "${RBRN_MONIKER}"; then
363	        z_offenders+=("${z_name}")
364	      elif test "${z_proj_label}" != "${z_proj_label%-"${RBRN_MONIKER}"}"; then
365	        z_offenders+=("${z_name}")
366	      fi
367	    done
368	  fi
369	
370	  test "${#z_offenders[@]}" -gt 0 || return 0
371	
372	  local z_msg="Nameplate '${RBRN_MONIKER}' collision: ${#z_offenders[@]} foreign compose network(s) match — clean up before charging:"
373	  local z_offender=""
374	  for z_offender in "${z_offenders[@]}"; do
375	    z_msg="${z_msg}"$'\n'"  docker rm \$(docker ps -aq --filter network=${z_offender}) 2>/dev/null; docker network rm ${z_offender}"
376	  done
377	  buc_die "${z_msg}"
378	}
379	
380	# Die if another Docker network already occupies this nameplate's subnet.
381	# Catches non-compose networks (manual `docker network create`) that the
382	# label-based scan above can't see. Our own network is excluded.
383	zrbob_detect_subnet_conflict() {
384	  zrbob_sentinel
385	
386	  local z_target_subnet="${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP}/${RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK}"
387	  local z_networks_file="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/zrbob_detect_networks.txt"
388	  local z_subnet_file="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/zrbob_detect_subnet.txt"
389	
390	  # List all Docker network names
391	  ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} network ls --format '{{.Name}}' > "${z_networks_file}" \
392	    || buc_die "Failed to list Docker networks"
393	
394	  # Load network names (load-then-iterate per BCG)
395	  local z_names=()
396	  while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
397	    z_names+=("${z_line}")
398	  done < "${z_networks_file}"
399	
400	  # Inspect each network's subnet
401	  local z_name=""
402	  for z_name in "${z_names[@]}"; do
403	    # Skip our own compose network — compose down already handled it
404	    test "${z_name}" != "${ZRBOB_NETWORK}" || continue
405	
406	    ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} network inspect "${z_name}" \
407	      --format '{{range .IPAM.Config}}{{.Subnet}}{{end}}' \
408	      > "${z_subnet_file}" 2>/dev/null || continue
409	
410	    local z_subnet=""
411	    z_subnet=$(<"${z_subnet_file}")
412	
413	    if test "${z_subnet}" = "${z_target_subnet}"; then
414	      buc_die "Subnet ${z_target_subnet} already claimed by stale network '${z_name}' — clean up manually: docker rm \$(docker ps -aq --filter network=${z_name}) 2>/dev/null; docker network rm ${z_name}"
415	    fi
416	  done
417	}
418	
419	######################################################################
420	# Public API
421	
422	# Start the crucible (sentry + pentacle + bottle) via compose
423	# Requires: RBOB kindled (which requires RBRN and RBRR)
424	rbob_charge() {
425	  zrbob_sentinel
426	
427	  buc_step "Starting crucible: ${RBRN_MONIKER}"
428	
429	  # Gate: nameplate must have no uncommitted changes
430	  if ! git diff --quiet -- "${ZRBOB_ENV_RBRN}" 2>/dev/null; then
431	    buc_die "Nameplate has uncommitted changes: ${ZRBOB_ENV_RBRN} — commit before charging"
432	  fi
433	
434	  # Cross-nameplate validation (silent on success, dies on conflict)
435	  rbrn_preflight
436	
437	  # Preflight: ensure all hallmark arks exist locally. Vouch presence is the
438	  # hallmark-level signal — if vouch is missing, the full hallmark is treated
439	  # as not-yet-summoned and all three arks are pulled together. Image-only
440	  # auto-summon below is defense-in-depth for partial-state cases.
441	  if ! ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} image inspect "${ZRBOB_SENTRY_VOUCH}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
442	    if zrbob_hallmark_is_kludge "${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}"; then
443	      buc_die "Kludge sentry hallmark not built locally: ${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}"
444	    fi
445	    buc_warn "Sentry vouch artifact missing locally: ${ZRBOB_SENTRY_VOUCH}"
446	    zrbob_summon_full_hallmark "${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}"
447	  fi
448	
449	  if ! ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} image inspect "${ZRBOB_BOTTLE_VOUCH}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
450	    if zrbob_hallmark_is_kludge "${RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK}"; then
451	      buc_die "Kludge bottle hallmark not built locally: ${RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK}"
452	    fi
453	    buc_warn "Bottle vouch artifact missing locally: ${ZRBOB_BOTTLE_VOUCH}"
454	    zrbob_summon_full_hallmark "${RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK}"
455	  fi
456	
457	  if ! ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} image inspect "${ZRBOB_SENTRY_IMAGE}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
458	    if zrbob_hallmark_is_kludge "${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}"; then
459	      buc_die "Kludge sentry hallmark not built locally: ${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}"
460	    fi
461	    buc_warn "Sentry image not found locally: ${ZRBOB_SENTRY_IMAGE}"
462	    zrbob_vouch_gate_and_summon "${RBRN_SENTRY_VESSEL}" "${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}" "${ZRBOB_SENTRY_IMAGE}"
463	  fi
464	
465	  if ! ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} image inspect "${ZRBOB_BOTTLE_IMAGE}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
466	    if zrbob_hallmark_is_kludge "${RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK}"; then
467	      buc_die "Kludge bottle hallmark not built locally: ${RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK}"
468	    fi
469	    buc_warn "Bottle image not found locally: ${ZRBOB_BOTTLE_IMAGE}"
470	    zrbob_vouch_gate_and_summon "${RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL}" "${RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK}" "${ZRBOB_BOTTLE_IMAGE}"
471	  fi
472	
473	  # Tear down any prior state (tolerates missing project)
474	  buc_step "Cleaning up any prior state"
475	  zrbob_compose --profile sessile down --remove-orphans 2>/dev/null || true
476	
477	  # Charge invariant: at most one crucible per nameplate per system. The
478	  # collision scan refuses on any foreign compose project whose network
479	  # bears our moniker (catches pre-prefix-era residue independent of the
480	  # current run's project name); the subnet scan catches non-compose
481	  # networks that bypass label-based detection.
482	  zrbob_detect_nameplate_collision
483	  zrbob_detect_subnet_conflict
484	
485	  # Start services via compose (--profile sessile includes bottle)
486	  # --wait blocks until all health checks pass (sentry → pentacle → bottle chain)
487	  buc_step "Starting services via compose"
488	  zrbob_compose --profile sessile up -d --wait
489	
490	  buc_step "Crucible started: ${RBRN_MONIKER}"
491	
492	  if test "${RBRN_BOTTLE_READINESS_DELAY_SEC}" -gt 0; then
493	    buc_step "Waiting ${RBRN_BOTTLE_READINESS_DELAY_SEC}s for bottle service readiness"
494	    sleep "${RBRN_BOTTLE_READINESS_DELAY_SEC}"
495	  fi
496	}
497	
498	# Check whether the crucible is charged (compose project has running containers).
499	# BCG predicate: returns 0 if charged, 1 if not. Never dies, no output.
500	rbob_charged_predicate() {
501	  zrbob_sentinel
502	  "${ZRBOB_RUNTIME}" compose -p "${ZRBOB_PROJECT}" ps -q --status running 2>/dev/null | grep -q .
503	}
504	
505	# Stop the crucible via compose
506	rbob_quench() {
507	  zrbob_sentinel
508	
509	  buc_step "Stopping crucible: ${RBRN_MONIKER}"
510	
511	  zrbob_compose --profile sessile down --remove-orphans
512	
513	  buc_step "Crucible stopped: ${RBRN_MONIKER}"
514	}
515	
516	# Hail sentry — call out to the guard (interactive shell)
517	rbob_hail() {
518	  zrbob_sentinel
519	  buc_step "Hailing sentry: ${ZRBOB_SENTRY}"
520	  exec ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} exec -it "${ZRBOB_SENTRY}" /bin/bash
521	}
522	
523	# SSH into bottle — proper terminal session via sentry DNAT
524	rbob_ssh() {
525	  zrbob_sentinel
526	  local z_ssh_user="${ZRBOB_BOTTLE_USER:-root}"
527	  buc_step "SSH to bottle via port ${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION} as ${z_ssh_user}"
528	  exec ssh -t -p "${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION}" \
529	    -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no -o UserKnownHostsFile=/dev/null \
530	    "${z_ssh_user}@localhost"
531	}
532	
533	# Rack bottle — compel the demon to reveal state (interactive shell)
534	rbob_rack() {
535	  zrbob_sentinel
536	  buc_step "Racking bottle: ${ZRBOB_BOTTLE}"
537	  exec ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} exec -it "${ZRBOB_BOTTLE}" /bin/bash
538	}
539	
540	# Writ sentry — non-interactive command execution in sentry
541	rbob_writ() {
542	  zrbob_sentinel
543	  buc_step "Writ to sentry: ${ZRBOB_SENTRY}"
544	  exec ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} exec "${ZRBOB_SENTRY}" "$@"
545	}
546	
547	# Fiat pentacle — non-interactive command execution in pentacle
548	rbob_fiat() {
549	  zrbob_sentinel
550	  buc_step "Fiat to pentacle: ${ZRBOB_PENTACLE}"
551	  exec ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} exec "${ZRBOB_PENTACLE}" "$@"
552	}
553	
554	# Bark bottle — non-interactive command execution in bottle
555	rbob_bark() {
556	  zrbob_sentinel
557	  buc_step "Bark to bottle: ${ZRBOB_BOTTLE}"
558	  exec ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} exec "${ZRBOB_BOTTLE}" "$@"
559	}
560	
561	# Launch Claude Code client inside bottle (ifrit engagement)
562	rbob_ifrit_client() {
563	  zrbob_sentinel
564	  buc_step "Launching Claude Code client in bottle: ${ZRBOB_BOTTLE}"
565	  exec ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} exec -it -w "${RBRR_BOTTLE_WORKSPACE}" "${ZRBOB_BOTTLE}" claude
566	}
567	
568	# Run ifrit sortie adjutant inside bottle (security test dispatch)
569	rbob_ifrit_sortie() {
570	  zrbob_sentinel
571	  buc_step "Running ifrit sortie adjutant in bottle: ${ZRBOB_BOTTLE}"
572	  ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} exec -w "${RBRR_BOTTLE_WORKSPACE}" "${ZRBOB_BOTTLE}" \
573	    python3 rbtia_adjutant.py "$@"
574	}
575	
576	######################################################################
577	# Drive Hallmark — rewrite a single RBRN_*_HALLMARK line in nameplate env
578	# Args: nameplate_file variable_name new_hallmark
579	
580	zrbob_drive_hallmark() {
581	  local -r z_file="$1"
582	  local -r z_var_name="$2"
583	  local -r z_new_value="$3"
584	
585	  test -f "${z_file}" || buc_die "Nameplate file not found: ${z_file}"
586	  test -n "${z_var_name}" || buc_die "Variable name required"
587	  test -n "${z_new_value}" || buc_die "New hallmark value required"
588	
589	  # Load file into array (BCG load-then-iterate)
590	  local z_lines=()
591	  while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
592	    z_lines+=("${z_line}")
593	  done < "${z_file}"
594	
595	  # Rewrite with substitution
596	  local -r z_temp="${ZRBOB_DRIVE_PREFIX}${z_var_name}"
597	  : > "${z_temp}" || buc_die "Failed to create temp file: ${z_temp}"
598	
599	  local z_found=0
600	  local z_i="" z_current=""
601	  for z_i in "${!z_lines[@]}"; do
602	    z_current="${z_lines[$z_i]}"
603	    case "${z_current}" in
604	      "${z_var_name}"=*)
605	        z_current="${z_var_name}=${z_new_value}"
606	        z_found=1
607	        ;;
608	    esac
609	    printf '%s\n' "${z_current}" >> "${z_temp}" || buc_die "Failed to write line"
610	  done
611	
612	  test "${z_found}" = "1" || buc_die "Variable ${z_var_name} not found in ${z_file}"
613	
614	  # Atomic replace
615	  mv "${z_temp}" "${z_file}" || buc_die "Failed to replace nameplate file: ${z_file}"
616	
617	  buc_info "Drove ${z_var_name}=${z_new_value} → ${z_file}"
618	}
619	
620	######################################################################
621	# Kludge Bottle — param1 dispatch target delegating to rbob_kludge
622	
623	rbob_kludge_bottle() {
624	  zrbob_sentinel
625	  rbob_kludge
626	}
627	
628	######################################################################
629	# Kludge — build bottle vessel locally and drive hallmark into nameplate
630	
631	rbob_kludge() {
632	  zrbob_sentinel
633	  zrbfc_sentinel
634	
635	  buc_doc_brief "Build bottle vessel locally and drive hallmark into nameplate"
636	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
637	
638	  buc_step "Kludge: ${RBRN_MONIKER} (${RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL})"
639	
640	  # Delegate build to foundry kludge — override BUZ_FOLIO with the vessel sigil
641	  # for the duration of the call (rbfd_kludge reads BUZ_FOLIO as its operand).
642	  BUZ_FOLIO="${RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL}" rbfd_kludge
643	
644	  # Read hallmark from fact file
645	  local z_hallmark=""
646	  z_hallmark=$(<"${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/${RBF_FACT_HALLMARK}") \
647	    || buc_die "Failed to read hallmark from kludge output"
648	  test -n "${z_hallmark}" || buc_die "Empty hallmark from kludge output"
649	
650	  # Drive hallmark into nameplate
651	  zrbob_drive_hallmark "${ZRBOB_ENV_RBRN}" "RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK" "${z_hallmark}"
652	
653	  buc_success "Kludge installed: ${z_hallmark} → ${RBRN_MONIKER}"
654	}
655	
656	######################################################################
657	# Kludge Sentry — build sentry vessel locally and drive hallmark into nameplate
658	
659	rbob_kludge_sentry() {
660	  zrbob_sentinel
661	  zrbfc_sentinel
662	
663	  buc_doc_brief "Build sentry vessel locally and drive hallmark into nameplate"
664	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
665	
666	  buc_step "Kludge: ${RBRN_MONIKER} (${RBRN_SENTRY_VESSEL})"
667	
668	  BUZ_FOLIO="${RBRN_SENTRY_VESSEL}" rbfd_kludge
669	
670	  # Read hallmark from fact file
671	  local z_hallmark=""
672	  z_hallmark=$(<"${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/${RBF_FACT_HALLMARK}") \
673	    || buc_die "Failed to read hallmark from kludge output"
674	  test -n "${z_hallmark}" || buc_die "Empty hallmark from kludge output"
675	
676	  # Drive hallmark into nameplate
677	  zrbob_drive_hallmark "${ZRBOB_ENV_RBRN}" "RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK" "${z_hallmark}"
678	
679	  buc_success "Kludge installed: ${z_hallmark} → ${RBRN_MONIKER}"
680	}
681	
682	######################################################################
683	# Ordain — cloud-build bottle vessel and drive hallmark into nameplate
684	
685	rbob_ordain() {
686	  zrbob_sentinel
687	  zrbfd_sentinel
688	
689	  buc_doc_brief "Ordain bottle vessel via cloud build and drive hallmark into nameplate"
690	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
691	
692	  buc_step "Ordain: ${RBRN_MONIKER} (${RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL})"
693	
694	  BUZ_FOLIO="${RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL}" rbfd_ordain
695	
696	  # Read hallmark from fact file
697	  local z_hallmark=""
698	  z_hallmark=$(<"${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/${RBF_FACT_HALLMARK}") \
699	    || buc_die "Failed to read hallmark from ordain output"
700	  test -n "${z_hallmark}" || buc_die "Empty hallmark from ordain output"
701	
702	  # Drive hallmark into nameplate
703	  zrbob_drive_hallmark "${ZRBOB_ENV_RBRN}" "RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK" "${z_hallmark}"
704	
705	  buc_success "Ordain installed: ${z_hallmark} → ${RBRN_MONIKER}"
706	}
707	
708	# eof
709

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2025 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# RBOB - Recipe Bottle Orchestration Bottle
# Container lifecycle management via Docker Compose
#
# Requires: buc_command.sh sourced
# Requires: rbrn_regime.sh sourced
# Requires: rbrr_regime.sh sourced

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion detection
test -z "${ZRBOB_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbob multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
ZRBOB_SOURCED=1


######################################################################
# Compose-env Validation

# Validate that compose-consumed RBRN fields contain no quotes or ${VAR} references.
# Compose --env-file does not strip quotes (they become part of the value) and
# interpolates ${VAR} references (causing unintended expansion). Fields not consumed
# by compose (e.g., RBRN_DESCRIPTION) are exempt.
zrbob_validate_compose_env() {
  # Explicit list of compose-consumed field names (the nameplate↔compose contract)
  local z_compose_fields="
    RBRN_MONIKER
    RBRN_RUNTIME
    RBRN_SENTRY_VESSEL
    RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL
    RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK
    RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK
    RBRN_ENTRY_MODE
    RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION
    RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE
    RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP
    RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK
    RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP
    RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP
    RBRN_UPLINK_PORT_MIN
    RBRN_UPLINK_DNS_MODE
    RBRN_UPLINK_ACCESS_MODE
    RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_CIDRS
    RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_DOMAINS
  "

  local z_field=""
  for z_field in ${z_compose_fields}; do
    local z_value="${!z_field:-}"
    # Check for quotes (compose does not strip them)
    case "${z_value}" in
      *\"*|*\'*)
        buc_die "Compose compatibility: ${z_field} contains quotes — compose --env-file does not strip quotes, they become part of the value"
        ;;
    esac
    # Check for ${VAR} references (compose interpolates them)
    case "${z_value}" in
      *'${'*)
        buc_die "Compose compatibility: ${z_field} contains \${VAR} reference — compose --env-file interpolates these, causing unintended expansion"
        ;;
    esac
  done
}

######################################################################
# Kindle and Sentinel

zrbob_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZRBOB_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbob already kindled"

  # Verify RBRN regime is kindled (provides nameplate config)
  zrbrn_sentinel

  # Validate compose-consumed fields before proceeding
  zrbob_validate_compose_env

  # Verify RBRR regime is kindled (provides repo config like DNS_SERVER)
  zrbrr_sentinel

  # Runtime command (docker or podman)
  case "${RBRN_RUNTIME}" in
    docker) readonly ZRBOB_RUNTIME="docker" ;;
    podman) readonly ZRBOB_RUNTIME="podman" ;;
    *) buc_die "Unknown RBRN_RUNTIME: ${RBRN_RUNTIME}" ;;
  esac

  # Compose project identity for this nameplate. The runtime-prefixed moniker
  # is the authoritative project name compose labels containers and networks
  # under; every -p invocation and every container/network name derives from it.
  readonly ZRBOB_PROJECT="${RBRR_RUNTIME_PREFIX}${RBRN_MONIKER}"

  # Container names (for connect and info commands)
  readonly ZRBOB_SENTRY="${ZRBOB_PROJECT}-sentry"
  readonly ZRBOB_PENTACLE="${ZRBOB_PROJECT}-pentacle"
  readonly ZRBOB_BOTTLE="${ZRBOB_PROJECT}-bottle"

  # Network name (compose names as {project}_{network}; used by observe and info)
  readonly ZRBOB_NETWORK="${ZRBOB_PROJECT}_enclave"

  # Compose file paths (relative to project root, where compose runs)
  readonly ZRBOB_COMPOSE_BASE="${RBBC_dot_dir}/rbob_compose.yml"
  test -f "${ZRBOB_COMPOSE_BASE}" || buc_die "Base compose file not found: ${ZRBOB_COMPOSE_BASE}"

  readonly ZRBOB_COMPOSE_FRAGMENT="${RBBC_dot_dir}/${RBRN_MONIKER}/compose.yml"
  # Fragment is optional — existence checked at compose invocation time

  # Env file paths (for compose --env-file: YAML interpolation + container env forwarding)
  readonly ZRBOB_ENV_RBRR="${RBBC_dot_dir}/rbrr.env"
  readonly ZRBOB_ENV_RBJE="${RBBC_dot_dir}/rbje_compose_probe.env"
  readonly ZRBOB_ENV_RBRN="${RBBC_dot_dir}/${RBRN_MONIKER}/${RBCC_rbrn_file}"

  # RBDC env file — RBDC_* are bash-kindle constants invisible to compose
  # without an env-file bridge. Write the subset compose interpolates into
  # a temp file and feed it via --env-file.
  local z_rbdc_env="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbob_rbdc_compose.env"
  printf 'RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID=%s\nRBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY=%s\n' \
    "${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}" "${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY}" > "${z_rbdc_env}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to write RBDC compose env file: ${z_rbdc_env}"
  readonly ZRBOB_ENV_RBDC="${z_rbdc_env}"


  # GAR image references (computed once, used by preflight and auto-summon)
  local z_gar_base="${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}/${RBGD_GAR_PROJECT_ID}/${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY}"
  readonly ZRBOB_SENTRY_IMAGE="${z_gar_base}/${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}/${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_IMAGE}:${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}"
  readonly ZRBOB_BOTTLE_IMAGE="${z_gar_base}/${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/${RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK}/${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_IMAGE}:${RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK}"

  # GAR vouch references (local presence verified on every start)
  readonly ZRBOB_SENTRY_VOUCH="${z_gar_base}/${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}/${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_VOUCH}:${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}"
  readonly ZRBOB_BOTTLE_VOUCH="${z_gar_base}/${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/${RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK}/${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_VOUCH}:${RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK}"

  # Export RBRN/RBRR vars for compose environment: bare name forwarding.
  # Compose --env-file populates the compose environment for both YAML interpolation
  # and bare name forwarding; explicit exports provide defense-in-depth.
  export RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP
  export RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK
  export RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP
  export RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP
  export RBRN_ENTRY_MODE
  export RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION
  export RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE
  export RBRN_UPLINK_DNS_MODE
  export RBRN_UPLINK_PORT_MIN
  export RBRN_UPLINK_ACCESS_MODE
  export RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_CIDRS
  export RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_DOMAINS
  export RBRR_DNS_SERVER
  export RBRR_BOTTLE_WORKSPACE

  # Host UID/GID for bind-mount ownership alignment.
  # The person running charge IS the host identity — no configuration needed.
  local z_uid_file="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbob_host_uid"
  local z_gid_file="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbob_host_gid"
  id -u > "${z_uid_file}" || buc_die "Failed to determine host UID"
  id -g > "${z_gid_file}" || buc_die "Failed to determine host GID"
  local z_host_uid=""
  z_host_uid=$(<"${z_uid_file}")
  local z_host_gid=""
  z_host_gid=$(<"${z_gid_file}")
  export RBOB_HOST_UID="${z_host_uid}"
  export RBOB_HOST_GID="${z_host_gid}"

  # Load bottle vessel user for compose and SSH (optional — empty means image default)
  local z_bottle_rbrv="${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}/${RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL}/rbrv.env"
  local z_bottle_user=""
  if test -f "${z_bottle_rbrv}"; then
    z_bottle_user=$(grep '^RBRV_USER=' "${z_bottle_rbrv}" | head -1 | cut -d= -f2) || true
  fi
  readonly ZRBOB_BOTTLE_USER="${z_bottle_user}"
  export RBRV_USER="${z_bottle_user}"

  readonly ZRBOB_DRIVE_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbob_drive_"

  readonly ZRBOB_KINDLED=1
}

zrbob_sentinel() {
  test "${ZRBOB_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbob not kindled - call zrbob_kindle first"
}

######################################################################
# Compose Command Helper

# Build and execute a compose command with standard args
# Usage: zrbob_compose <compose_subcommand> [args...]
zrbob_compose() {
  zrbob_sentinel

  local z_args=()
  z_args+=("compose")
  z_args+=("--env-file" "${ZRBOB_ENV_RBRR}")
  z_args+=("--env-file" "${ZRBOB_ENV_RBDC}")
  z_args+=("--env-file" "${ZRBOB_ENV_RBJE}")
  z_args+=("--env-file" "${ZRBOB_ENV_RBRN}")
  z_args+=("-f" "${ZRBOB_COMPOSE_BASE}")

  # Include nameplate fragment if it exists
  if test -f "${ZRBOB_COMPOSE_FRAGMENT}"; then
    z_args+=("-f" "${ZRBOB_COMPOSE_FRAGMENT}")
  fi

  z_args+=("-p" "${ZRBOB_PROJECT}")
  z_args+=("$@")

  buc_log_args "${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} ${z_args[*]}"
  "${ZRBOB_RUNTIME}" "${z_args[@]}"
}

######################################################################
# Auto-Summon Helpers

# Discriminate kludge hallmarks (local-only, no GAR presence) from conjure /
# bind / graft hallmarks. Used by charge preflight to redirect the diagnostic
# away from the summon path, which is guaranteed to fail for kludge stamps.
zrbob_hallmark_is_kludge() {
  case "${1}" in
    "${RBGC_HALLMARK_PREFIX_KLUDGE}"*) return 0 ;;
    *) return 1 ;;
  esac
}

# Pull all three arks (image, about, vouch) for a hallmark to local runtime.
# Used when charge detects a missing local artifact and needs to bootstrap.
# Mirrors rbfr_summon's all-three-arks semantics; called inline rather than
# as a tabtarget invocation because rbob is the sole caller and avoids the
# launcher-dispatch round-trip.
zrbob_summon_full_hallmark() {
  local z_hallmark="${1:-}"

  test -n "${z_hallmark}" || buc_die "zrbob_summon_full_hallmark: hallmark required"

  local z_gar_base="${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}/${RBGD_GAR_PROJECT_ID}/${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY}"
  local z_image_ref="${z_gar_base}/${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/${z_hallmark}/${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_IMAGE}:${z_hallmark}"
  local z_about_ref="${z_gar_base}/${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/${z_hallmark}/${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_ABOUT}:${z_hallmark}"
  local z_vouch_ref="${z_gar_base}/${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/${z_hallmark}/${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_VOUCH}:${z_hallmark}"

  local z_registry_host="${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}"
  test -f "${RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE}" || buc_die "Retriever credential not found: ${RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE}"

  local z_token
  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE}") || buc_die "Failed to get OAuth token for auto-summon"

  buc_step "Auto-summoning hallmark ${z_hallmark} (image + about + vouch)"

  echo "${z_token}" | docker login -u oauth2accesstoken --password-stdin "https://${z_registry_host}" \
    || buc_die "Container registry authentication failed during auto-summon"

  docker pull "${z_image_ref}" || buc_die "Failed to pull image ark: ${z_image_ref}"
  docker pull "${z_about_ref}" || buc_die "Failed to pull about ark: ${z_about_ref}"
  docker pull "${z_vouch_ref}" || buc_die "Failed to pull vouch ark: ${z_vouch_ref}"

  buc_info "Auto-summoned: ${z_hallmark}"
}

# Pull a single image ref to local runtime — used by the image-only auto-summon
# branch in rbob_charge as defense-in-depth for partial-state cases (vouch
# present but image missing). The full-hallmark helper above covers the common
# path where all arks are missing together.
# Usage: zrbob_vouch_gate_and_summon <vessel> <hallmark> <image_ref>
zrbob_vouch_gate_and_summon() {
  local z_vessel="${1:-}"
  local z_hallmark="${2:-}"
  local z_image_ref="${3:-}"

  test -n "${z_vessel}"       || buc_die "zrbob_vouch_gate_and_summon: vessel required"
  test -n "${z_hallmark}" || buc_die "zrbob_vouch_gate_and_summon: hallmark required"
  test -n "${z_image_ref}"    || buc_die "zrbob_vouch_gate_and_summon: image_ref required"

  local z_registry_host="${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}"
  test -f "${RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE}" || buc_die "Retriever credential not found: ${RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE}"

  local z_token
  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE}") || buc_die "Failed to get OAuth token for auto-summon"

  # Pull the image
  buc_step "Auto-summoning ${z_image_ref}"

  echo "${z_token}" | docker login -u oauth2accesstoken --password-stdin "https://${z_registry_host}" \
    || buc_die "Container registry authentication failed during auto-summon"

  docker pull "${z_image_ref}" || buc_die "Failed to pull image: ${z_image_ref}"

  buc_info "Auto-summoned: ${z_image_ref}"
}

######################################################################
# Charge Invariant — Network Collision Detection

# Die if any compose-managed network with our nameplate exists from a foreign
# project. Catches pre-prefix-era residue (project=tadmor without RBRR
# prefix) and any other compose project whose name is the bare moniker or
# ends with "-{moniker}". Match is by labels — independent of how compose
# joined project + network into the network name.
zrbob_detect_nameplate_collision() {
  zrbob_sentinel

  local -r z_networks_file="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/zrbob_collision_networks.txt"
  local -r z_networks_stderr="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/zrbob_collision_networks_stderr.txt"
  local -r z_inspect_prefix="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/zrbob_collision_inspect_"

  ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} network ls \
    --filter 'label=com.docker.compose.project' \
    --filter 'label=com.docker.compose.network' \
    --format '{{.Name}}' \
    > "${z_networks_file}" 2>"${z_networks_stderr}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to list Docker networks for collision detection — see ${z_networks_stderr}"

  local z_names=()
  local z_line=""
  while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
    test -n "${z_line}" || continue
    z_names+=("${z_line}")
  done < "${z_networks_file}"

  local z_offenders=()
  local z_index=0
  local z_name=""
  local z_inspect_file=""
  local z_inspect_stderr=""
  local z_labels=""
  local z_net_label=""
  local z_proj_label=""

  if (( ${#z_names[@]} )); then
    for z_name in "${z_names[@]}"; do
      z_inspect_file="${z_inspect_prefix}${z_index}.txt"
      z_inspect_stderr="${z_inspect_prefix}${z_index}_stderr.txt"
      z_index=$((z_index + 1))

      ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} network inspect "${z_name}" \
        --format '{{index .Labels "com.docker.compose.network"}}|{{index .Labels "com.docker.compose.project"}}' \
        > "${z_inspect_file}" 2>"${z_inspect_stderr}" \
        || continue

      z_labels=$(<"${z_inspect_file}")
      z_net_label="${z_labels%%|*}"
      z_proj_label="${z_labels#*|}"

      # Network must be enclave or transit
      test "${z_net_label}" = "enclave" || test "${z_net_label}" = "transit" \
        || continue

      # Skip our own current project; compose-down already removed it (defensive)
      test "${z_proj_label}" != "${ZRBOB_PROJECT}" || continue

      # Match: bare moniker, or any prefix ending in "-{moniker}"
      if test "${z_proj_label}" = "${RBRN_MONIKER}"; then
        z_offenders+=("${z_name}")
      elif test "${z_proj_label}" != "${z_proj_label%-"${RBRN_MONIKER}"}"; then
        z_offenders+=("${z_name}")
      fi
    done
  fi

  test "${#z_offenders[@]}" -gt 0 || return 0

  local z_msg="Nameplate '${RBRN_MONIKER}' collision: ${#z_offenders[@]} foreign compose network(s) match — clean up before charging:"
  local z_offender=""
  for z_offender in "${z_offenders[@]}"; do
    z_msg="${z_msg}"$'\n'"  docker rm \$(docker ps -aq --filter network=${z_offender}) 2>/dev/null; docker network rm ${z_offender}"
  done
  buc_die "${z_msg}"
}

# Die if another Docker network already occupies this nameplate's subnet.
# Catches non-compose networks (manual `docker network create`) that the
# label-based scan above can't see. Our own network is excluded.
zrbob_detect_subnet_conflict() {
  zrbob_sentinel

  local z_target_subnet="${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP}/${RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK}"
  local z_networks_file="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/zrbob_detect_networks.txt"
  local z_subnet_file="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/zrbob_detect_subnet.txt"

  # List all Docker network names
  ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} network ls --format '{{.Name}}' > "${z_networks_file}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to list Docker networks"

  # Load network names (load-then-iterate per BCG)
  local z_names=()
  while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
    z_names+=("${z_line}")
  done < "${z_networks_file}"

  # Inspect each network's subnet
  local z_name=""
  for z_name in "${z_names[@]}"; do
    # Skip our own compose network — compose down already handled it
    test "${z_name}" != "${ZRBOB_NETWORK}" || continue

    ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} network inspect "${z_name}" \
      --format '{{range .IPAM.Config}}{{.Subnet}}{{end}}' \
      > "${z_subnet_file}" 2>/dev/null || continue

    local z_subnet=""
    z_subnet=$(<"${z_subnet_file}")

    if test "${z_subnet}" = "${z_target_subnet}"; then
      buc_die "Subnet ${z_target_subnet} already claimed by stale network '${z_name}' — clean up manually: docker rm \$(docker ps -aq --filter network=${z_name}) 2>/dev/null; docker network rm ${z_name}"
    fi
  done
}

######################################################################
# Public API

# Start the crucible (sentry + pentacle + bottle) via compose
# Requires: RBOB kindled (which requires RBRN and RBRR)
rbob_charge() {
  zrbob_sentinel

  buc_step "Starting crucible: ${RBRN_MONIKER}"

  # Gate: nameplate must have no uncommitted changes
  if ! git diff --quiet -- "${ZRBOB_ENV_RBRN}" 2>/dev/null; then
    buc_die "Nameplate has uncommitted changes: ${ZRBOB_ENV_RBRN} — commit before charging"
  fi

  # Cross-nameplate validation (silent on success, dies on conflict)
  rbrn_preflight

  # Preflight: ensure all hallmark arks exist locally. Vouch presence is the
  # hallmark-level signal — if vouch is missing, the full hallmark is treated
  # as not-yet-summoned and all three arks are pulled together. Image-only
  # auto-summon below is defense-in-depth for partial-state cases.
  if ! ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} image inspect "${ZRBOB_SENTRY_VOUCH}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if zrbob_hallmark_is_kludge "${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}"; then
      buc_die "Kludge sentry hallmark not built locally: ${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}"
    fi
    buc_warn "Sentry vouch artifact missing locally: ${ZRBOB_SENTRY_VOUCH}"
    zrbob_summon_full_hallmark "${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}"
  fi

  if ! ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} image inspect "${ZRBOB_BOTTLE_VOUCH}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if zrbob_hallmark_is_kludge "${RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK}"; then
      buc_die "Kludge bottle hallmark not built locally: ${RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK}"
    fi
    buc_warn "Bottle vouch artifact missing locally: ${ZRBOB_BOTTLE_VOUCH}"
    zrbob_summon_full_hallmark "${RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK}"
  fi

  if ! ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} image inspect "${ZRBOB_SENTRY_IMAGE}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if zrbob_hallmark_is_kludge "${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}"; then
      buc_die "Kludge sentry hallmark not built locally: ${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}"
    fi
    buc_warn "Sentry image not found locally: ${ZRBOB_SENTRY_IMAGE}"
    zrbob_vouch_gate_and_summon "${RBRN_SENTRY_VESSEL}" "${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}" "${ZRBOB_SENTRY_IMAGE}"
  fi

  if ! ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} image inspect "${ZRBOB_BOTTLE_IMAGE}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if zrbob_hallmark_is_kludge "${RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK}"; then
      buc_die "Kludge bottle hallmark not built locally: ${RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK}"
    fi
    buc_warn "Bottle image not found locally: ${ZRBOB_BOTTLE_IMAGE}"
    zrbob_vouch_gate_and_summon "${RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL}" "${RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK}" "${ZRBOB_BOTTLE_IMAGE}"
  fi

  # Tear down any prior state (tolerates missing project)
  buc_step "Cleaning up any prior state"
  zrbob_compose --profile sessile down --remove-orphans 2>/dev/null || true

  # Charge invariant: at most one crucible per nameplate per system. The
  # collision scan refuses on any foreign compose project whose network
  # bears our moniker (catches pre-prefix-era residue independent of the
  # current run's project name); the subnet scan catches non-compose
  # networks that bypass label-based detection.
  zrbob_detect_nameplate_collision
  zrbob_detect_subnet_conflict

  # Start services via compose (--profile sessile includes bottle)
  # --wait blocks until all health checks pass (sentry → pentacle → bottle chain)
  buc_step "Starting services via compose"
  zrbob_compose --profile sessile up -d --wait

  buc_step "Crucible started: ${RBRN_MONIKER}"

  if test "${RBRN_BOTTLE_READINESS_DELAY_SEC}" -gt 0; then
    buc_step "Waiting ${RBRN_BOTTLE_READINESS_DELAY_SEC}s for bottle service readiness"
    sleep "${RBRN_BOTTLE_READINESS_DELAY_SEC}"
  fi
}

# Check whether the crucible is charged (compose project has running containers).
# BCG predicate: returns 0 if charged, 1 if not. Never dies, no output.
rbob_charged_predicate() {
  zrbob_sentinel
  "${ZRBOB_RUNTIME}" compose -p "${ZRBOB_PROJECT}" ps -q --status running 2>/dev/null | grep -q .
}

# Stop the crucible via compose
rbob_quench() {
  zrbob_sentinel

  buc_step "Stopping crucible: ${RBRN_MONIKER}"

  zrbob_compose --profile sessile down --remove-orphans

  buc_step "Crucible stopped: ${RBRN_MONIKER}"
}

# Hail sentry — call out to the guard (interactive shell)
rbob_hail() {
  zrbob_sentinel
  buc_step "Hailing sentry: ${ZRBOB_SENTRY}"
  exec ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} exec -it "${ZRBOB_SENTRY}" /bin/bash
}

# SSH into bottle — proper terminal session via sentry DNAT
rbob_ssh() {
  zrbob_sentinel
  local z_ssh_user="${ZRBOB_BOTTLE_USER:-root}"
  buc_step "SSH to bottle via port ${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION} as ${z_ssh_user}"
  exec ssh -t -p "${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION}" \
    -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no -o UserKnownHostsFile=/dev/null \
    "${z_ssh_user}@localhost"
}

# Rack bottle — compel the demon to reveal state (interactive shell)
rbob_rack() {
  zrbob_sentinel
  buc_step "Racking bottle: ${ZRBOB_BOTTLE}"
  exec ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} exec -it "${ZRBOB_BOTTLE}" /bin/bash
}

# Writ sentry — non-interactive command execution in sentry
rbob_writ() {
  zrbob_sentinel
  buc_step "Writ to sentry: ${ZRBOB_SENTRY}"
  exec ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} exec "${ZRBOB_SENTRY}" "$@"
}

# Fiat pentacle — non-interactive command execution in pentacle
rbob_fiat() {
  zrbob_sentinel
  buc_step "Fiat to pentacle: ${ZRBOB_PENTACLE}"
  exec ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} exec "${ZRBOB_PENTACLE}" "$@"
}

# Bark bottle — non-interactive command execution in bottle
rbob_bark() {
  zrbob_sentinel
  buc_step "Bark to bottle: ${ZRBOB_BOTTLE}"
  exec ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} exec "${ZRBOB_BOTTLE}" "$@"
}

# Launch Claude Code client inside bottle (ifrit engagement)
rbob_ifrit_client() {
  zrbob_sentinel
  buc_step "Launching Claude Code client in bottle: ${ZRBOB_BOTTLE}"
  exec ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} exec -it -w "${RBRR_BOTTLE_WORKSPACE}" "${ZRBOB_BOTTLE}" claude
}

# Run ifrit sortie adjutant inside bottle (security test dispatch)
rbob_ifrit_sortie() {
  zrbob_sentinel
  buc_step "Running ifrit sortie adjutant in bottle: ${ZRBOB_BOTTLE}"
  ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} exec -w "${RBRR_BOTTLE_WORKSPACE}" "${ZRBOB_BOTTLE}" \
    python3 rbtia_adjutant.py "$@"
}

######################################################################
# Drive Hallmark — rewrite a single RBRN_*_HALLMARK line in nameplate env
# Args: nameplate_file variable_name new_hallmark

zrbob_drive_hallmark() {
  local -r z_file="$1"
  local -r z_var_name="$2"
  local -r z_new_value="$3"

  test -f "${z_file}" || buc_die "Nameplate file not found: ${z_file}"
  test -n "${z_var_name}" || buc_die "Variable name required"
  test -n "${z_new_value}" || buc_die "New hallmark value required"

  # Load file into array (BCG load-then-iterate)
  local z_lines=()
  while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
    z_lines+=("${z_line}")
  done < "${z_file}"

  # Rewrite with substitution
  local -r z_temp="${ZRBOB_DRIVE_PREFIX}${z_var_name}"
  : > "${z_temp}" || buc_die "Failed to create temp file: ${z_temp}"

  local z_found=0
  local z_i="" z_current=""
  for z_i in "${!z_lines[@]}"; do
    z_current="${z_lines[$z_i]}"
    case "${z_current}" in
      "${z_var_name}"=*)
        z_current="${z_var_name}=${z_new_value}"
        z_found=1
        ;;
    esac
    printf '%s\n' "${z_current}" >> "${z_temp}" || buc_die "Failed to write line"
  done

  test "${z_found}" = "1" || buc_die "Variable ${z_var_name} not found in ${z_file}"

  # Atomic replace
  mv "${z_temp}" "${z_file}" || buc_die "Failed to replace nameplate file: ${z_file}"

  buc_info "Drove ${z_var_name}=${z_new_value} → ${z_file}"
}

######################################################################
# Kludge Bottle — param1 dispatch target delegating to rbob_kludge

rbob_kludge_bottle() {
  zrbob_sentinel
  rbob_kludge
}

######################################################################
# Kludge — build bottle vessel locally and drive hallmark into nameplate

rbob_kludge() {
  zrbob_sentinel
  zrbfc_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Build bottle vessel locally and drive hallmark into nameplate"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  buc_step "Kludge: ${RBRN_MONIKER} (${RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL})"

  # Delegate build to foundry kludge — override BUZ_FOLIO with the vessel sigil
  # for the duration of the call (rbfd_kludge reads BUZ_FOLIO as its operand).
  BUZ_FOLIO="${RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL}" rbfd_kludge

  # Read hallmark from fact file
  local z_hallmark=""
  z_hallmark=$(<"${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/${RBF_FACT_HALLMARK}") \
    || buc_die "Failed to read hallmark from kludge output"
  test -n "${z_hallmark}" || buc_die "Empty hallmark from kludge output"

  # Drive hallmark into nameplate
  zrbob_drive_hallmark "${ZRBOB_ENV_RBRN}" "RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK" "${z_hallmark}"

  buc_success "Kludge installed: ${z_hallmark} → ${RBRN_MONIKER}"
}

######################################################################
# Kludge Sentry — build sentry vessel locally and drive hallmark into nameplate

rbob_kludge_sentry() {
  zrbob_sentinel
  zrbfc_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Build sentry vessel locally and drive hallmark into nameplate"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  buc_step "Kludge: ${RBRN_MONIKER} (${RBRN_SENTRY_VESSEL})"

  BUZ_FOLIO="${RBRN_SENTRY_VESSEL}" rbfd_kludge

  # Read hallmark from fact file
  local z_hallmark=""
  z_hallmark=$(<"${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/${RBF_FACT_HALLMARK}") \
    || buc_die "Failed to read hallmark from kludge output"
  test -n "${z_hallmark}" || buc_die "Empty hallmark from kludge output"

  # Drive hallmark into nameplate
  zrbob_drive_hallmark "${ZRBOB_ENV_RBRN}" "RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK" "${z_hallmark}"

  buc_success "Kludge installed: ${z_hallmark} → ${RBRN_MONIKER}"
}

######################################################################
# Ordain — cloud-build bottle vessel and drive hallmark into nameplate

rbob_ordain() {
  zrbob_sentinel
  zrbfd_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Ordain bottle vessel via cloud build and drive hallmark into nameplate"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  buc_step "Ordain: ${RBRN_MONIKER} (${RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL})"

  BUZ_FOLIO="${RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL}" rbfd_ordain

  # Read hallmark from fact file
  local z_hallmark=""
  z_hallmark=$(<"${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/${RBF_FACT_HALLMARK}") \
    || buc_die "Failed to read hallmark from ordain output"
  test -n "${z_hallmark}" || buc_die "Empty hallmark from ordain output"

  # Drive hallmark into nameplate
  zrbob_drive_hallmark "${ZRBOB_ENV_RBRN}" "RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK" "${z_hallmark}"

  buc_success "Ordain installed: ${z_hallmark} → ${RBRN_MONIKER}"
}

# eof

file.content:
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
#
# Recipe Bottle base compose file — static infrastructure
#
# Invoked via:
#   docker compose --env-file .rbk/rbrr.env --env-file .rbk/{moniker}/rbrn.env \
#     -f .rbk/rbob_compose.yml [-f .rbk/{moniker}/compose.yml] -p {moniker} up -d
#
# Env-file variables serve dual purpose:
#   1. YAML interpolation (${VAR} in this file — image refs, subnet, container names)
#   2. Container environment forwarding (bare names in environment: sections)
#
# Network topology:
#   sentry: transit (uplink to internet via bridge) + enclave (down to bottle)
#     Interface naming and default-route assignment are NOT controlled by compose:
#       - eth0/eth1 ordering is Docker-engine-defined (sentry IP-discovers via RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP)
#       - default route is assigned to the alphabetically-first attached network's gateway
#         (compose 'priority:' keys empirically do NOT influence this on linux Docker
#         engine 28.x or mac Docker Desktop 28.x — sentry replaces the default route
#         with one via uplink at RBJp1, see rbjs_sentry.sh)
#   pentacle: enclave only (static IP, routes through sentry)
#   bottle: shares pentacle network namespace (network_mode: service:pentacle)
#
# Health chain: sentry healthy → pentacle starts → pentacle healthy → bottle starts (sessile profile)

services:
  sentry:
    image: ${RBRR_GCP_REGION}-docker.pkg.dev/${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}/${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY}/rbi_hm/${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}/image:${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}
    container_name: ${RBRR_RUNTIME_PREFIX}${RBRN_MONIKER}-sentry
    privileged: true
    networks:
      transit: {}
      enclave:
        ipv4_address: ${RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP}
    ports:
      - "${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION}:${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION}"
    environment:
      - RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP
      - RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK
      - RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP
      - RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP
      - RBRR_DNS_SERVER
      - RBRN_ENTRY_MODE
      - RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION
      - RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE
      - RBRN_UPLINK_DNS_MODE
      - RBRN_UPLINK_PORT_MIN
      - RBRN_UPLINK_ACCESS_MODE
      - RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_CIDRS
      - RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_DOMAINS
      - RBJE_PROBE
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "test -f /tmp/rbjh_healthy"]
      interval: 2s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 15
      start_period: 5s

  pentacle:
    image: ${RBRR_GCP_REGION}-docker.pkg.dev/${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}/${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY}/rbi_hm/${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}/image:${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}
    container_name: ${RBRR_RUNTIME_PREFIX}${RBRN_MONIKER}-pentacle
    privileged: true
    networks:
      enclave:
        ipv4_address: ${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP}
    environment:
      - RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP
    command: ["/opt/rbk/rbjp_pentacle.sh"]
    depends_on:
      sentry:
        condition: service_healthy
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "test -f /tmp/rbjph_healthy"]
      interval: 2s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 15
      start_period: 5s

  bottle:
    image: ${RBRR_GCP_REGION}-docker.pkg.dev/${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}/${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY}/rbi_hm/${RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK}/image:${RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK}
    container_name: ${RBRR_RUNTIME_PREFIX}${RBRN_MONIKER}-bottle
    network_mode: "service:pentacle"
    security_opt:
      - label:disable
    cap_drop:
      - NET_RAW
      - NET_ADMIN
    environment:
      - RBOB_HOST_UID
      - RBOB_HOST_GID
      - RBRV_USER
    depends_on:
      pentacle:
        condition: service_healthy
    profiles:
      - sessile

networks:
  transit:
    driver: bridge
  enclave:
    driver: bridge
    ipam:
      config:
        - subnet: ${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP}/${RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK}

1	# Debian bookworm-slim for minimal trust boundary sentry
2	ARG RBF_IMAGE_1
3	FROM ${RBF_IMAGE_1}
4	
5	# Avoid prompts from apt
6	ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
7	
8	# Install sentry operational packages + pentacle diagnostic packages
9	# (pentacle container shares this image for trusted network observation)
10	RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
11	    bash            `# Shell for sentry setup script`       \
12	    iptables        `# Firewall rule configuration`         \
13	    dnsmasq         `# DNS forwarding for enclave`          \
14	    iproute2        `# Network configuration (ip command)`  \
15	    iputils-ping    `# ICMP tests (pentacle role)`            \
16	    dnsutils        `# dig/nslookup (pentacle role)`          \
17	    procps          `# pidof/ps for process inspection`       \
18	    tcpdump         `# Network probe diagnostic (scry)`       \
19	    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
20	
21	# Pin iptables-legacy backend (nftables not available in slim)
22	RUN update-alternatives --set iptables /usr/sbin/iptables-legacy
23	
24	# Bake entrypoint scripts (sentry setup + pentacle routing)
25	# Source of truth: Tools/rbk/rbjs_sentry.sh, Tools/rbk/rbjp_pentacle.sh
26	# Changes require deliberate build→vouch cycle
27	COPY rbjs_sentry.sh  /opt/rbk/rbjs_sentry.sh
28	COPY rbjp_pentacle.sh /opt/rbk/rbjp_pentacle.sh
29	RUN chmod +x /opt/rbk/rbjs_sentry.sh /opt/rbk/rbjp_pentacle.sh
30	
31	# Default: sentry entrypoint (pentacle overrides via compose command)
32	CMD ["/opt/rbk/rbjs_sentry.sh"]
33

file.content:
# Debian bookworm-slim for minimal trust boundary sentry
ARG RBF_IMAGE_1
FROM ${RBF_IMAGE_1}

# Avoid prompts from apt
ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

# Install sentry operational packages + pentacle diagnostic packages
# (pentacle container shares this image for trusted network observation)
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
    bash            `# Shell for sentry setup script`       \
    iptables        `# Firewall rule configuration`         \
    dnsmasq         `# DNS forwarding for enclave`          \
    iproute2        `# Network configuration (ip command)`  \
    iputils-ping    `# ICMP tests (pentacle role)`            \
    dnsutils        `# dig/nslookup (pentacle role)`          \
    procps          `# pidof/ps for process inspection`       \
    tcpdump         `# Network probe diagnostic (scry)`       \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Pin iptables-legacy backend (nftables not available in slim)
RUN update-alternatives --set iptables /usr/sbin/iptables-legacy

# Bake entrypoint scripts (sentry setup + pentacle routing)
# Source of truth: Tools/rbk/rbjs_sentry.sh, Tools/rbk/rbjp_pentacle.sh
# Changes require deliberate build→vouch cycle
COPY rbjs_sentry.sh  /opt/rbk/rbjs_sentry.sh
COPY rbjp_pentacle.sh /opt/rbk/rbjp_pentacle.sh
RUN chmod +x /opt/rbk/rbjs_sentry.sh /opt/rbk/rbjp_pentacle.sh

# Default: sentry entrypoint (pentacle overrides via compose command)
CMD ["/opt/rbk/rbjs_sentry.sh"]

1	#!/bin/sh
2	#
3	# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
4	#
5	# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
6	# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
7	# You may obtain a copy of the License at
8	#
9	#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
10	#
11	# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
12	# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
13	# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
14	# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
15	# limitations under the License.
16	#
17	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
18	#
19	# RBJP - Pentacle initialization script
20	# Configures network routing through sentry for enclave isolation.
21	# Baked into sentry image at /opt/rbk/rbjp_pentacle.sh.
22	#
23	# Requires: RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP in container environment
24	
25	set -e
26	
27	echo "RBJP: Beginning pentacle setup"
28	
29	echo "RBJP: Validate parameters"
30	: "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP:?}" && echo "RBJP: RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP = ${RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP}"
31	
32	echo "RBJP: Configuring DNS to use sentry"
33	echo "nameserver ${RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP}" > /etc/resolv.conf || exit 10
34	
35	echo "RBJP: Discovering enclave interface (single non-loopback interface expected)"
36	z_temp_file="/tmp/rbjp_iface_discovery.txt"
37	ip -o -4 addr show scope global > "${z_temp_file}" || exit 11
38	read z_num RBJP_ENCLAVE_IF z_rest < "${z_temp_file}"
39	rm -f "${z_temp_file}"
40	test -n "${RBJP_ENCLAVE_IF}" || { echo "RBJP: FATAL - No enclave interface found"; exit 11; }
41	echo "RBJP: Enclave interface = ${RBJP_ENCLAVE_IF}"
42	
43	echo "RBJP: Flushing ARP entries"
44	ip link set ${RBJP_ENCLAVE_IF} down && ip link set ${RBJP_ENCLAVE_IF} up && ip -s -s neigh flush all || exit 20
45	
46	echo "RBJP: Setting default route through sentry"
47	ip route add default via "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP}" || exit 30
48	
49	echo "RBJP: Verifying default route"
50	ip route | grep -q "^default via ${RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP}" || { echo "RBJP: FATAL - default route not set"; exit 31; }
51	
52	echo "RBJP: Signaling health"
53	touch /tmp/rbjph_healthy || exit 40
54	
55	echo "RBJP: Pentacle setup complete, entering hold"
56	exec sleep infinity

file.content:
#!/bin/sh
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# RBJP - Pentacle initialization script
# Configures network routing through sentry for enclave isolation.
# Baked into sentry image at /opt/rbk/rbjp_pentacle.sh.
#
# Requires: RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP in container environment

set -e

echo "RBJP: Beginning pentacle setup"

echo "RBJP: Validate parameters"
: "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP:?}" && echo "RBJP: RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP = ${RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP}"

echo "RBJP: Configuring DNS to use sentry"
echo "nameserver ${RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP}" > /etc/resolv.conf || exit 10

echo "RBJP: Discovering enclave interface (single non-loopback interface expected)"
z_temp_file="/tmp/rbjp_iface_discovery.txt"
ip -o -4 addr show scope global > "${z_temp_file}" || exit 11
read z_num RBJP_ENCLAVE_IF z_rest < "${z_temp_file}"
rm -f "${z_temp_file}"
test -n "${RBJP_ENCLAVE_IF}" || { echo "RBJP: FATAL - No enclave interface found"; exit 11; }
echo "RBJP: Enclave interface = ${RBJP_ENCLAVE_IF}"

echo "RBJP: Flushing ARP entries"
ip link set ${RBJP_ENCLAVE_IF} down && ip link set ${RBJP_ENCLAVE_IF} up && ip -s -s neigh flush all || exit 20

echo "RBJP: Setting default route through sentry"
ip route add default via "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP}" || exit 30

echo "RBJP: Verifying default route"
ip route | grep -q "^default via ${RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP}" || { echo "RBJP: FATAL - default route not set"; exit 31; }

echo "RBJP: Signaling health"
touch /tmp/rbjph_healthy || exit 40

echo "RBJP: Pentacle setup complete, entering hold"
exec sleep infinity

{scr_iptables_init}

Establishes foundational security state:

//axhos_step
. Base Chain Configuration:
* Flush all rules from filter and nat tables
* Set policy DROP on all base chains (INPUT, FORWARD, OUTPUT)
* Accept loopback interface traffic unconditionally via first rule in INPUT chain

//axhos_step
. State Tracking Setup:
* Insert RELATED,ESTABLISHED tracking as second rule in chains:
** INPUT chain with "-m state --state RELATED,ESTABLISHED -j ACCEPT"
** FORWARD chain with same state match
** OUTPUT chain with same state match

//axhos_step
. RBM Chain Creation:
* Create in filter table with default policy DROP:
** RBM-INGRESS for input control
** RBM-EGRESS for output control
** RBM-FORWARD for forwarding control
* Insert jumps as third rule in respective base chains:
** INPUT jumps to RBM-INGRESS
** OUTPUT jumps to RBM-EGRESS
** FORWARD jumps to RBM-FORWARD

content.content:
1	//axhob_operation
2	{scr_port_setup}
3	
4	If {rbrn_entry_mode} is disabled, this sequence is skipped.
5	If {rbrn_entry_mode} is enabled, this sequence configures entry port forwarding using kernel-level iptables DNAT.
6	No userspace proxy is involved; the kernel forwards packets directly.
7	
8	//axhos_step
9	. IP Forwarding:
10	* Enable IPv4 forwarding via `/proc/sys/net/ipv4/ip_forward`
11	
12	//axhos_step
13	. DNAT Rule:
14	* Add PREROUTING rule in nat table:
15	** Input interface eth0 ({at_transit_network})
16	** Protocol TCP
17	** Destination port {rbrn_entry_port_workstation}
18	** DNAT to {rbrn_enclave_bottle_ip}:{rbrn_entry_port_enclave}
19	
20	//axhos_step
21	. Forward Filter:
22	* Add rule to RBM-FORWARD accepting DNATted entry traffic:
23	** Input interface eth0, output interface eth1
24	** Protocol TCP
25	** Destination {rbrn_enclave_bottle_ip}
26	** Destination port {rbrn_entry_port_enclave}
27	
28	//axhos_step
29	. MASQUERADE Rule:
30	* Add POSTROUTING rule in nat table:
31	** Output interface eth1 ({at_enclave_network})
32	** Protocol TCP
33	** Destination {rbrn_enclave_bottle_ip}
34	** Destination port {rbrn_entry_port_enclave}
35	** MASQUERADE (rewrites source to sentry enclave IP)
36	* Return traffic is handled by RELATED,ESTABLISHED tracking in the FORWARD chain ({scr_iptables_init} state tracking)

content.content:
1	//axhob_operation
2	{opss_sentry_start}
3	
4	NOTE: Docker Compose orchestrates the full container lifecycle declaratively.
5	The sentry starts first; its health check (`/tmp/rbjh_healthy`) gates the pentacle; the pentacle's health check (`/tmp/rbjph_healthy`) gates the bottle.
6	Network creation, container instantiation, and startup ordering are all managed by compose — the sub-operations below describe what happens inside each container at startup, not imperative orchestration steps.
7	
8	//axhos_step
9	. *Compose creates networks and starts sentry*:
10	.. Compose creates
11	{at_transit_network}
12	(`transit`) and
13	{at_enclave_network}
14	(`enclave` with static subnet from nameplate)
15	.. Sentry container starts on both networks with privileged mode
16	.. Baked-in entrypoint (`rbjs_sentry.sh`) executes
17	
18	//axhos_step
19	. *Sentry discovers interfaces and takes ownership of routing*:
20	.. Sentry uses `ip -o addr show to «enclave_ip»` to identify which interface carries the enclave IP
21	.. The other global-scope interface is identified as the uplink
22	.. All iptables rules use discovered interface names, not hardcoded eth0/eth1
23	.. NOTE: Docker Compose does not guarantee network-to-interface ordering; IP-based discovery is required
24	.. Sentry replaces the default route with one via the uplink gateway, computed as the first usable IP of the uplink subnet (Docker bridge convention)
25	.. NOTE: Docker assigns the default route to the alphabetically-first attached network's gateway (empirically, on both linux engine 28.x and mac Docker Desktop 28.x); compose `services.<svc>.networks.<net>.priority` does NOT influence this. Sentry-side ownership is the load-bearing fix — same don't-trust-Docker discipline that motivates IP-based interface discovery.
26	
27	//axhos_step
28	. *Sentry configures iptables*:
29	.. Default policies: DROP on INPUT, FORWARD, OUTPUT
30	.. Custom chains: RBM-INGRESS, RBM-EGRESS, RBM-FORWARD
31	.. ICMP allowed within enclave only
32	.. Entry port DNAT if enabled
33	.. Access and DNS rules per nameplate configuration (allowlist/global/disabled modes)
34	
35	//axhos_step
36	. *Sentry starts dnsmasq*:
37	.. Listens on enclave interface at
38	{rbrn_enclave_sentry_ip}
39	.. Domain filtering per
40	{rbrn_uplink_dns_mode}
41	(allowlist or global)
42	.. DNS firewall rules added to RBM-INGRESS and RBM-EGRESS
43	
44	//axhos_step
45	. *Sentry signals health*:
46	.. Creates `/tmp/rbjh_healthy`
47	.. Compose health check detects this and allows pentacle to start
48	
49	//axhoc_completion
50	After sentry is healthy, the
51	{at_sentry_container}
52	has dual network connectivity with iptables enforcement and DNS services active, ready to serve the
53	{rbsc_crucible}.
54

file.content:
//axhob_operation
{opss_sentry_start}

NOTE: Docker Compose orchestrates the full container lifecycle declaratively.
The sentry starts first; its health check (`/tmp/rbjh_healthy`) gates the pentacle; the pentacle's health check (`/tmp/rbjph_healthy`) gates the bottle.
Network creation, container instantiation, and startup ordering are all managed by compose — the sub-operations below describe what happens inside each container at startup, not imperative orchestration steps.

//axhos_step
. *Compose creates networks and starts sentry*:
.. Compose creates
{at_transit_network}
(`transit`) and
{at_enclave_network}
(`enclave` with static subnet from nameplate)
.. Sentry container starts on both networks with privileged mode
.. Baked-in entrypoint (`rbjs_sentry.sh`) executes

//axhos_step
. *Sentry discovers interfaces and takes ownership of routing*:
.. Sentry uses `ip -o addr show to «enclave_ip»` to identify which interface carries the enclave IP
.. The other global-scope interface is identified as the uplink
.. All iptables rules use discovered interface names, not hardcoded eth0/eth1
.. NOTE: Docker Compose does not guarantee network-to-interface ordering; IP-based discovery is required
.. Sentry replaces the default route with one via the uplink gateway, computed as the first usable IP of the uplink subnet (Docker bridge convention)
.. NOTE: Docker assigns the default route to the alphabetically-first attached network's gateway (empirically, on both linux engine 28.x and mac Docker Desktop 28.x); compose `services.<svc>.networks.<net>.priority` does NOT influence this. Sentry-side ownership is the load-bearing fix — same don't-trust-Docker discipline that motivates IP-based interface discovery.

//axhos_step
. *Sentry configures iptables*:
.. Default policies: DROP on INPUT, FORWARD, OUTPUT
.. Custom chains: RBM-INGRESS, RBM-EGRESS, RBM-FORWARD
.. ICMP allowed within enclave only
.. Entry port DNAT if enabled
.. Access and DNS rules per nameplate configuration (allowlist/global/disabled modes)

//axhos_step
. *Sentry starts dnsmasq*:
.. Listens on enclave interface at
{rbrn_enclave_sentry_ip}
.. Domain filtering per
{rbrn_uplink_dns_mode}
(allowlist or global)
.. DNS firewall rules added to RBM-INGRESS and RBM-EGRESS

//axhos_step
. *Sentry signals health*:
.. Creates `/tmp/rbjh_healthy`
.. Compose health check detects this and allows pentacle to start

//axhoc_completion
After sentry is healthy, the
{at_sentry_container}
has dual network connectivity with iptables enforcement and DNS services active, ready to serve the
{rbsc_crucible}.
